compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: INTEGRATION RECORD (2026-07-15). This epic was promoted from idea and structured per the operator handoff integrating docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (143d5074e) into the review-calibration work. Children: E1=e95e (corpora, kappa>=0.7 validity gate), E2=d125 (seeded-defect benchmark, pre-registered thresholds), E3=def5 (counterfactual replay ~200 reviews, ADR-0036 segmented), E5=5342 (cross-gate byte-identical replay), E6=a880 (judge reliability), R7=b1a4 (instrumentation, >=6/8 reproduction gate), R6=5dac (advisory-triage move, ship-first), R2=b080 (operator-attested DET lint, self-gated lexicon eval), R1=59b7 (capability probe, gated E2+E3), R3=3a27 (decomposition-shape, advisory-only, gated E2+E6), R4=03a9 (necessity probe + bug tier, retrospective gate), R5=ea39 (kernel sub-answer, gated E5+E6, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump). OPERATOR DIRECTIVES incorporated: (1) NO A/B testing - validation is eval-based, post-ship validation is dogfooding via R7 metrics, approach documented at each step; (2) NO config-flag/overlay gating - validated changes land directly in the packaged review process (posture stays a registry property; R3 permanently advisory). Sequencing vs the calibration-3 line: R3/R4/R5 depends_on the floor-split story large-sleepful-needlefish (shared criteria_routing.json / review_kernel / sidecar files); experiments E1-E6 have no file overlap and can run alongside. Empirical §5 claims are treated as UNVERIFIED until E1 re-derives them (16/505 edits; 3/8 misses = defect-class identification only, binomial 95% CI 9-76%).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
E1 OUTCOME + EPIC STATE (2026-07-16). E1 (e95e) is CLOSED + SIGNED, but RESCOPED: it delivered only its VALID parts — the outcome corpus (628 rows, event-mined) + mining harness + the SOTA §5 report + the reusable UNLABELED finding sample (landed via Gerrit 740+744). Its finding-level TP/FP adjudication corpus + kappa gate were INVALIDATED (the labeling was tool-less + plan-truncated, unlike the whole-plan/code-grounded/agentic production gate; ~79% contaminated, human layer included) and DESCOPED. The independent human-adjudicated gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5 (relates_to this epic).

IMPACT ON THE EPIC: dependents are UNBLOCKED on VALID foundations — E2(d125)/E3(def5)/R2(b080)/R7(b1a4) use the OUTCOME corpus; E6(a880) uses the finding SAMPLE (re-judged for self-consistency; never needed the labels); R1(59b7) uses §5 re-verification (still gated on E2+E3). So the invalidation did NOT block the epic — E1's valid deliverable satisfies the real needs. The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE's 'E1 kappa<0.7' example is now moot (kappa descoped).

WHAT REMAINS (per operator, budget-constrained: LLM spend minimized, human time over budget): the E-experiments (E2/E3/E5/E6) + R-implementations (R1-R7) are unrun/unimplemented — executing them is the deferred, resourcing-dependent work. METHODOLOGICAL LESSON (candidate ADR): an adjudicator must be at least as capable (grounded, whole-context) as the gate it judges, and re-running the gate's own verifier is circular — prefer CONSTRUCT-VALID ground truth (E2 seeded defects), the outcome corpus, and the gate's persisted sidecar self-assessment over LLM-adjudicated finding labels. Audit sibling experiments for the same tool-less/truncated confound before building on any labeled corpus. Full arc in the session log.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: R2 (b080) GROUNDING CORRECTION (2026-07-17): the epic Context/ordering says 'R2/R3/R4 each touch criteria_routing.json and land serially in any order (file-impact keeps them out of one batch)'. R2's implementation grounding REFUTES this for R2: the operator-attested evidence-kind lint is a P6-family static DET floor check (extends p6_ac_quality in det_floor.py). DET floor checks (P1-P9) are NOT routed through criteria_routing.json — registry.validate_packaged_routing() (registry.py:445) flags any routing key not in CANONICAL_LLM as an ORPHAN and fails CI, and det_invariants only consumes .rebar/-overlay exec:DET criteria backed by CODE grounding detectors (engine_b), which is structurally wrong for an AC-TEXT lexicon. So R2 does NOT touch criteria_routing.json (its file_impact is det_floor.py + workflow_ops.py + tests + docs/experiments artifacts + docs/plan-review-gate.md). The serialization-on-criteria_routing.json rationale still holds for R3/R4, but not for R2 — R2 has no criteria_routing.json file-conflict with them. (Flagged as plan-review G7 advisory on b080.)

compiled_state.description:
Integrate the task-decomposition SOTA research (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b) into the plan-review gate: validate each recommendation experimentally with evals FIRST, implement only behind its results gate, then dogfood in production review. Per operator direction (2026-07-15): no A/B testing - validation is eval-based (the E-gates below), post-ship observation is dogfooding via R7 instrumentation, and the approach must be clearly documented at every step.

## Context
GATE TERMS: each child R1-R7 story carries its own falsifiable pass/fail thresholds (drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table) INSIDE its Acceptance Criteria - the child ACs are the authoritative 'gate rows'; this epic does not duplicate the numbers. R6 is the one deliberate exception: a deterministic pass-4 coaching move with no detection behavior ships first with no eval gate (validated by dogfooding via R7 metrics). Experiment ids follow the report's numbering; E4 (the in-situ A/B experiment) is deliberately DROPPED per the operator's no-A/B directive, which is why the experiment set is E1/E2/E3/E5/E6. INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate (e.g. E1 kappa < 0.7) or returns an ambiguous result gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded - ambiguity never rounds up to a pass. ORDERING: first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 (instrumentation and the no-gate move), so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands; R2/R3/R4 each touch criteria_routing.json and land serially in any order (file-impact keeps them out of one batch); R1 does not depend on R5 (the probe is effective without the kernel sub-answer; R5 strengthens its verification later); R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps).
The report's §5 mined this store's own history: post-claim description edits on 16/505 claimed work tickets (3.2%), 15 substantive; of 8 with persisted reviews, 3 were review MISSES sharing one defect class (plan asserts a capability an existing module lacks: dc58, db7b, 5886) and 4 were advisories applied only post-claim (latency, not blindness). Small n: 3/8 identifies the defect CLASS (binomial 95% CI 9-76%), it is not a precise miss rate - child E1 re-verifies these claims before anything depends on them. §6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment. Implementation targets: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ (orchestrator, det_floor.py, passes.py, criteria_routing.json), src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate), src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md. Calibration assets in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ are extended, not replaced. This epic runs alongside the calibration-3 line (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly, story large-sleepful-needlefish); implementation children that touch criteria_routing.json/review_kernel sequence after the floor-split story to avoid file conflicts.

## Success Criteria
- Every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed before implementation; every rejected/deferred recommendation has the failing result recorded.
- The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression: validated changes are incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating - operator direction 2026-07-15); new criteria enter at advisory POSTURE first where their child's gate terms say so, with R7 dogfood monitoring: when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% (the **blocking-FP proxy** for a criterion is the fraction of that criterion's BLOCKING findings, over a trailing window of reviews, that are subsequently adjudicated false-positive / not-block-worthy — measured by R7's instrumentation against E1's committed finding-adjudication ground-truth corpus; 10% is the demotion threshold), its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change (precedent: calibration 3 demoted T5e to advisory on FP evidence, task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly; T3/T10 are the precedent for keeping routinely-ignored criteria non-blocking in the first place).
- The plan-review methodology remains documented: docs/plan-review-gate.md reflects every landed change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E1 ground-truth corpora are frozen and committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ with a versioned README (outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded).
- [ ] Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6 - no E4, see Context) is closed with its results committed as a docs/experiments/ writeup (the repo artifact is the criterion; ticket comments mirror it).
- [ ] Each recommendation child (R1-R7) is either landed through Gerrit with its own AC gate terms demonstrably passed (R6's terms are ship-first: deterministic rendering + validator compliance), or closed/deferred with the failing result recorded - no detection-bearing recommendation implemented without its eval gate.
- [ ] All landed criteria/rubric changes are incorporated directly into the packaged registry/rubrics (no config-flag or overlay gating), with R7 dogfood monitoring documented, and docs/plan-review-gate.md updated to match.
compiled_state.title: Implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 from task-decomposition SOTA report
